meta.site_description: 9 Midsummer Ct — Gaithersburg, MD 20878. 4 bedrooms, 3.5 baths, 3,464 sq ft. Listed at $1,099,900. Spectacular renovation, fabulous floorplan, and possibly the best location in Washingtonian Woods backing to parkland, pond, and walking paths! description:
Set on a quiet cul-de-sac in Washingtonian Woods and backing to parkland, pond views, and walking paths, this extensively renovated home pairs a strong floorplan with a setting that feels notably private. Hardwood floors begin in the entry foyer and continue throughout the main level, where the rooms flow easily from one gathering space to the next.

The main level is designed for both entertaining and everyday comfort. A gracious living room with crown molding, recessed lighting, and a wood-burning masonry fireplace opens through double French doors to the expansive tiered deck, while the dining room is framed by natural views, custom built-ins, and polished designer lighting. At the center of the home, the renovated kitchen stands out with granite countertops, a substantial island, custom cabinetry, stainless steel appliances, a double-door pantry, and a dedicated coffee station. Just beyond, the family room is filled with natural light from skylights and oversized windows and is anchored by a double-sided fireplace. A private office with built-ins and a beautifully updated powder room complete the main level.

Upstairs, the primary suite offers the scale and flexibility of a true retreat, with a vaulted bedroom, a sitting room or dressing area, a generous walk-in closet, and multiple additional closets for exceptional storage. The renovated primary bath brings together marble and quartz finishes, custom cabinetry, a glass-enclosed shower, a linen closet, and refined fixtures and lighting. Three additional bedrooms are bright and well proportioned, served by an updated hall bath with custom tile surround and wide-plank LVP flooring. The upper-level laundry room adds another layer of convenience with a utility sink, folding counter, hanging rod, and extra cabinetry.

The finished lower level extends the home with a large recreation room, another wood-burning fireplace, a custom bar, a flexible game or lounge area, a spacious bonus room, a renovated full bath, and abundant storage, with direct access to the patio and backyard. Outside, the tiered Trex deck, patio, and welcoming front porch create multiple places to relax, entertain, and take in the surrounding greenery. Recent improvements including a newer roof, solar panels, an electric fence, and major system updates add substance to a home that is as practical as it is polished.
